trade.summary: Saudi Arabia is one of China's largest energy suppliers and a major trade partner. China imports Saudi crude oil (largest volume supplier). China exports electronics, machinery, vehicles, and consumer goods. China is Saudi Arabia's largest trading partner, with bilateral trade deepening under Vision 2030 industrialization.
disputes: Saudi-US alliance means alignment constraints on Saudi-China deepening
Competition between Chinese and Western tech for Saudi 5G and digital projects

diplomatic.summary: A landmark strategic relationship deepened by China's brokering of the Saudi-Iran normalization agreement (March 2023). President Xi visited Riyadh (December 2022) and secured extensive cooperation agreements. Saudi Arabia is engaging China as a counterbalance to US pressure on human rights and regional issues. Both coordinate in OPEC+ and other forums. Saudi Arabia values China's non-interference principle.
recent_incidents: Saudi Arabia announced interest in Yuan-denominated oil settlements
Saudi Arabia applied to join BRICS (2023, accepted for 2024)

military.summary: No formal military alliance—Saudi Arabia's security partnership with the US remains primary. However, China has sold ballistic missiles and military drones to Saudi Arabia. Saudi Arabia is acquiring Chinese military technology in parallel to US arms. Joint military cooperation is growing but limited by the Saudi-US security relationship.
incidents: China supplied DF-3 ballistic missiles to Saudi Arabia (1988)
China sold CH-4 armed drones and Wing Loong UAVs to Saudi Arabia
China reportedly assisted Saudi Arabia in building ballistic missile production facility

financial.summary: Saudi Arabia's PIF sovereign wealth fund is increasing Chinese investments. Saudi Aramco has Chinese joint ventures. China's state banks provide project financing for Vision 2030 projects. Saudi Arabia is considering RMB-denominated oil trade, though decision pending. Bilateral financial ties expanding rapidly.

energy.summary: Energy is the central pillar of the relationship. Saudi Arabia is China's largest or second-largest crude oil supplier. China is Saudi Arabia's largest oil customer, accounting for over 20% of Saudi exports. Saudi Aramco has signed long-term supply agreements and joint refining ventures in China. The relationship is deeply energy-driven.

scientific.summary: Growing scientific cooperation under Vision 2030. KAUST has partnerships with Chinese universities. China provides technology for Saudi digital economy and smart city projects (Neom). Joint publications in energy and materials science increasing.
